newsBriefing: In a day marked by shifting leadership and critical roster updates, the sports world saw Duke’s Cameron Boozer headline the AP All-America team as the only unanimous first-team selection, while the NFL Players Association elected former offensive lineman JC Tretter as its next executive director. On the international stage, Team USA secured first place in the FIBA Women’s World Cup Qualifying Tournament behind an MVP performance from Caitlin Clark, who successfully returned from a groin injury alongside senior national team debutants Paige Bueckers and Angel Reese. However, the injury bug bit the NHL hard as the Edmonton Oilers announced superstar Leon Draisaitl will miss the remainder of the regular season with a lower-body injury, and Tiger Woods admitted his status for next month's Masters remains unclear due to ongoing health fluctuations. Meanwhile, NASCAR took a firm stance on conduct by suspending driver Daniel Dye for mocking a fellow competitor’s sexuality, and Maxx Crosby addressed the fallout of his nixed trade to Baltimore by reiterating his lifelong commitment to the Raiders.
